questions.optionB: Chlamydia trachomatis serotypes A, B, Ba, C (leading infectious cause of blindness — follows FISTO classification: Follicles, Intense inflammation, Scarring, Trichiasis, Opacity)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Trachoma = Chlamydia trachomatis = leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. Why correct: Serotypes A, B, Ba, C (ocular); D-K causes inclusion conjunctivitis/genital infections. WHO FISTO classification: TF (follicular), TI (intense), TS (scarring), TT (trichiasis), CO (corneal opacity). Endemic in poor communities with water scarcity. Why A wrong: N. gonorrhoeae → ophthalmia neonatorum (purulent neonatal conjunctivitis). Why C wrong: Adenovirus → epidemic keratoconjunctivitis (EKC) / pharyngoconjunctival fever. Why D wrong: Staphylococcus → blepharitis, chronic bacterial conjunctivitis. Clinical relevance: SAFE strategy (WHO): Surgery for trichiasis, Antibiotics (azithromycin mass drug administration), Facial cleanliness, Environmental improvement (water, sanitation). Pathology: Herbert's pits (inactive follicles at limbus) = pathognomonic. Exam trap: Arlt's line = upper tarsal scarring. Herbert's follicles/pits = limbal follicles/inactive scars. Memory aid: 'Trachoma = Track marks of Chlamydia on upper tarsal conjunctiva.' Reference: Khurana – Conjunctiva.
questions.conceptTag: Trachoma Chlamydia FISTO SAFE infectious blindness
